result.actor_summary: APT28 conducted a large-scale DNS hijacking campaign targeting insecure MikroTik and TP-Link SOHO routers since at least May 2025, modifying router settings to establish malicious infrastructure for cyber espionage purposes. This operation demonstrates a shift toward compromising consumer-grade networking equipment as a persistence and reconnaissance mechanism, leveraging weak security configurations in widely deployed devices to maintain access and control over victim network traffic.
result.landscape_summary: This campaign reflects an accelerating trend of state-sponsored actors targeting consumer-grade network appliances as persistent infrastructure for large-scale DNS hijacking operations, exploiting the minimal security posture and long uptimes of SOHO devices relative to hardened enterprise targets. The shift toward router compromise as a platform for espionage infrastructure demonstrates APT28's adaptation to defend against endpoint-focused detection strategies by establishing foothold persistence at the network perimeter where visibility and attribution are typically lower.
result.title: Russian State-Linked APT28 Exploits SOHO Routers in Global DNS Hijacking Campaign
